hero_subtitle: SFTY connects multi-provider identity checks into a unified compliance layer that preserves onboarding evidence and supports audit-ready decision workflows for digital asset businesses.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Onboarding audit trails break across systems
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to preserve complete evidence from multiple identity verification providers during onboarding. This creates gaps in audit records that slow review, complicate explanations and weaken traceability for regulatory demands.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects identity verification outputs, routing decisions, reviewer actions and completed checks into one unified audit trail so compliance teams maintain full traceability and clear evidence for onboarding reviews.
workflow_title: From onboarding checks to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY connects identity verification outputs with risk-based routing, case workflows and decision logs to preserve structured audit evidence for onboarding reviews.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns fragmented identity checks into structured onboarding workflows with linked decisions, preserved audit evidence and clear case history for improved compliance control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with controlled orchestration of identity verification, preserving audit-ready evidence and strengthening bank due diligence within enterprise-grade deployment environments.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for onboarding audit trails?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance layer that connects identity verification outputs into structured onboarding workflows, preserving decision records and audit evidence for regulated digital asset businesses like VASPs.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure onboarding workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY orchestrates onboarding by connecting provider outputs, applying internal rules, routing reviews, opening cases, and recording decisions for clear, auditable identity verification processes.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves onboarding evidence by maintaining detailed decision logs, provider results, and supporting documents within each case to support audit readiness and compliance review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect multiple KYC providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY can integrate multiple KYC and KYB providers, normalizing their outputs into a connected workflow without disrupting existing systems or vendor relationships.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing KYC or KYB providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC or KYB providers but connects and structures their outputs into a controlled, auditable compliance operating layer.
final_title: Preserve onboarding evidence with structured workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects identity verification outputs into controlled cases and audit-ready onboarding records for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
